coverage_summary: The improved test cases show significant progress, addressing many previous gaps such as removing duplicates, adding boundary tests (TC004, TC005), race condition test (TC008), and edge cases (TC009, TC010, TC011). However, coverage remains incomplete for email format validation, password complexity, account verification, and security testing.
remaining_gaps: Email format validation (e.g., missing @, invalid domain, special characters) not tested
Password complexity requirements (uppercase, lowercase, digits, special characters) not specified or tested
Account creation confirmation/verification process (e.g., email verification link) not tested
Security tests missing: SQL injection, XSS, brute-force protection, rate limiting
Error handling for all validation failures not fully covered (e.g., specific error messages for empty fields, whitespace-only password)
Very long password or email boundary tests not included
No test for successful account creation with email containing special characters (if allowed)
recommendations: Add test cases for email format validation once requirements are clarified (e.g., invalid formats like 'user@', '@domain.com', 'user@domain')
Add test cases for password complexity once specified (e.g., must contain uppercase, lowercase, digit, special character)
Add test cases for account verification process (e.g., email with verification link, account activation)
Add security test cases: SQL injection in email/password fields, XSS in email field, brute-force attempt detection
Add boundary test for very long email (e.g., 254 characters) and very long password (e.g., 128 characters)
Clarify expected results in TC001 and TC004 by specifying exact confirmation message and redirect URL based on actual requirements
Consider adding a test for successful account creation with email containing allowed special characters (e.g., '+', '.')
